docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Chetan Doddamani

Senior Data Analyst

Research interest: Transportation planning, Travel behavior analysis, Statitsical modelling, multi-modal travel patterns, vehicle ownership patterns, activity-based demand modelling, and residential mobility decisions | GIS | Remote Sensing | Python

docs.title: About Me
docs.text: I am a Transportation engineer and panning researcher with 8+ years of experience in data-analysis, travel demand modeling, public transit planning, and multi-modal travel patterns. I have published 7 journal articles and presented my work at 10 international conferences. Proficiency in statistics and machine learning tools for strategic modelling. Currently at IISc, Bangalore I’m working on a real-world large scalable database to forecast Bangalore city travel demand to understand realistic development (infrastructure, fare, economic etc.) scenarios. Alongside, I use International Classification of Activities for Time-Use Statistics (ICATUS) to predict people’s time investment in various activities. On-going academic credit course: Foundational Mathematics for using GenAI at IISc Bangalore

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
• Proficiency

    • R, Python, QGIS, ArcGIS
    • Econometric and Statistical Modelling
    • Spatial Data Analysis
    • Acitivty-based Travel Analaysis

  • Programming

    • Python - API keys
    • R — sf, terra, ggplot2

  • Machine Learning Courses

    • CCE course at IISc: Foundational Mathematics for GenAI, May 2026 to August 2026
    • Project: Synthetic Daily Activity-Travel Schedule Generation Using India's Time Use Survey (TUS) 2024

  • Web Mapping & Data

    • Leaflet.js, Folium, MapLibre GL JS
    • Cloud storage — AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats — GeoTIFF, GeoParquet, NetCDF
    • Streamlit for data-driven web apps

  • Data & Cloud

    • PostgreSQL + PostGIS
    • Cloud storage: AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage
    • Data formats: GeoJSON, GeoTIFF, NetCDF, Zarr, GeoParquet

  • Drone / UAV Data Processing

    • Mission planning and flight operations
    • Photogrammetry: Agisoft Metashape, OpenDroneMap
    • Point cloud processing: CloudCompare, PDAL

Download CV

docs.title: Work Experience
docs.text:
Post Doctoral Research Associate-III — CiSTUP, IISc Bangalore

Nov 2024 – May 2026 | Bangalore, India

  • Designed and executed large-scale travel behavior surveys, ensuring methodological rigor and high-quality data collection across multiple locations.
  • Developed automated R and Python scripts for data validation, consistency checks, and preprocessing, enabling reliable downstream econometric and behavioral analysis.
  • Applied advanced QGIS techniques to geocode survey responses, strategically map survey locations, and generate spatial visualizations to support survey planning and empirical analysis.
  • Project report writing, communication and co-ordination to generate and business plans.
Researcher — Nagoya University, Japan

Jan 2024 – Jul 2024 | Nagoya, Japan

  • Last mile EV charging behavior analysis.
  • Analyzed vehicle ownership patterns by engine capacity in Japan.
  • Data curation to reveal why and when people are likely to relinquish EV ownership.
  • Developed R and Python workflows for data cleaning, validation, and exploratory statistical analysis.
Associate Transport Planner — Directorate of Urban Land Transport (DULT), Bangalore

Jun 2023 – Dec 2023 | Bangalore, India

  • Led data-driven evaluation of BRTS implementation and urban bus service performance, identifying operational bottlenecks and reliability issues relevant to large-scale network optimization.
  • Contributed to city logistics planning and Comprehensive City Mobility Plans (CMPs) by integrating travel demand data, service performance indicators, and accessibility metrics.
  • Translated analytical findings into actionable policy recommendations by engaging with senior government officials.
Senior Associate Consultant, Transportation Systems — ITDP

Mar 2022 – Oct 2022 | India

  • Applied data analytics and AI-based techniques to review and synthesize public transport and informal transport (IPT) problem statements across 45 Indian cities.
  • Developed analytical frameworks to assess system performance, operational gaps, and electrification readiness across heterogeneous urban contexts.
  • Prepared technical reports on scaling electric three-wheeler systems, integrating operational data, demand patterns, and policy constraints.
  • Designed and executed IPT survey instruments to collect real-world data on e-rickshaw procurement, operational challenges, and adoption barriers.
Junior Research Fellow — Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore

Sept 2015 – Jul 2016 | Bangalore, India

  • Data collection of pedestrian behavior, traffic survey, and parking survey.
  • Data collection of pedestrian behavior dynamics towards a big data approach – Kumbh Mela Experiment.
Assistant Professor — Kolhapur Institute of Technology, Kolhapur

Jan 2015 – Aug 2015 | Kolhapur, India

  • Subjects taught: Transportation Engineering, Urban Transportation Systems, Urban and Regional Planning.

docs.title: Journal Articles
docs.text:
1. Hanni, U., Yamamoto, T., Doddamani, C., & Nakamura, T. (2026). Analysis of Travel Activity Patterns and Charging Choices of BEV Users in Japan. (Accepted May 2026) Transportation, Springer.

  2. Doddamani, C., & Yamamoto, T. (2025). Analysis of the Factors Influencing the Household Car, Motorcycle, and Bicycle Ownership in Japan. Sustainable Transport and Livability, Taylor and Francis.

  3. Doddamani, C., Manoj, M., & Yamamoto, T. (2025). Effects of residential relocation and modal shift on travel satisfaction: context of Hubli-Dharwad cities in India. Sustainable Transport and Livability, Taylor and Francis.

  4. Doddamani, C., Majumder, P., & Manoj, M. (2024). Analysis of household car and two-wheeler ownership considering spatial dependency. Transportation Research Part D: Transport and Environment, 133, 104314.

  5. Doddamani, C., & Manoj, M. (2022). Residential relocation and changes in household vehicle ownership and travel behavior: Exploring the context of Hubli-Dharwad twin-cities in India from a planning viewpoint. (Impact factor: 6.615)

  6. Doddamani, C., & Manoj, M. (2021). Analysis of the influences of built environment measures on household car and motorcycle ownership decisions in Hubli-Dharwad cities. Transportation, Springer, 1–39. (Impact factor: 6.052)

  7. Doddamani, C., Manoj, M., & Maurya, Y. (2021). Geographical scale of residential relocation and its impacts on vehicle ownership and travel behavior. Journal of Transport Geography, 94, 103124. (Impact factor: 5.899)

  8. Doddamani, C., & Manoj, M. (2021). Effects of the Built Environment and Socio-demographics on the Car and Two-Wheeler Ownership Levels: A Case Study of Dharwad City. Transportation in Developing Economies, 7(2), 1–13. (Impact factor: 0.75)

docs.title: Conference Papers & Presentations
docs.text:
1. Pinjari, A. R., Rambha, T., Koushik, A., Hanni, C. K., Agarwal, P., …, Doddamani, C., et al. (2025). Aggregate-M3DM: A City-Scale Multi-Modal Mobility Demand Modelling Tool for Indian Cities. 8th Conference of Transportation Research Group of India (CTRG-2025), 17–20 December, Guwahati, India.

  2. Sarangi, P., & Doddamani, C. (2025). Exploring Gendered Interactions in In-home and Out-of-home Activity Participation: A Bayesian Analysis of NTUS Data 2019. 8th Conference of Transportation Research Group of India (CTRG-2025), 17–20 December, Guwahati, India.

  3. Doddamani, C., Manoj, M., & Yamamoto, T. (2025). Effects of residential relocation and modal shift on travel satisfaction: context of Hubli-Dharwad cities in India. 1st World Symposium on Sustainable Transport and Livability, 25–27 June, IISc Bangalore. 2nd Prize Award.

  4. Doddamani, C., & Yamamoto, T. (2025). Comparative Analysis of Factors Influencing Types of Household Car, Motorcycle, and Bicycle Ownership in Japan. Transportation Research Board Annual Meeting (104th TRB), 5–9 January, Washington, D.C., USA.

  5. Doddamani, C., & Manoj, M. (2022). Residential Relocation and Shopping Mode Switch Behavior: The Context of Hubli-Dharwad Cities. Transportation Research Board Annual Meeting (101st TRB), 9–13 January, Washington, D.C., USA.

  6. Doddamani, C., & Manoj, M. (2019). Effects of the Built Environment and Socio-demographics on the Car and Two-Wheeler Ownership Levels: A Case Study of Dharwad City. 5th Conference of Transportation Research Group of India (CTRG-2019).

  7. Doddamani, C., & Manoj, M. (2020). Housing tenure and vehicle ownership: A case study of Hubli-Dharwad twin cities. 13th International Conference on Transportation Planning and Implementation Methodologies for Developing Countries (TPMDC-2020), 10–11 December.

  8. Gulzar, S., Ali, H., & Doddamani, C. (2017). Ant colony optimization in pavement asset management. Proceedings of ASCE India Conference 2017.

docs.title: Sample Notebook
docs.text:
In [1]: Copied! 
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import folium
import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import folium In [ ]: Copied! 
# Placeholder time-series data
# Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv")
data = {
    "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
              "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"],
    "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118],
    "metric_b": [ 80,  88,  95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100,  92,  85,  78],
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df.head()
# Placeholder time-series data # Replace with: df = pd.read_csv("your_data.csv") data = { "month": ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"], "metric_a": [120, 135, 148, 162, 175, 190, 185, 178, 160, 145, 130, 118], "metric_b": [ 80, 88, 95, 105, 112, 120, 118, 110, 100, 92, 85, 78], } df = pd.DataFrame(data) df.head() In [ ]: Copied! 
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe()
df[["metric_a", "metric_b"]].describe() In [4]: Copied! 
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))

ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue")
ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral")

ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold")
ax.set_xlabel("Month")
ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4)) ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_a"], marker="o", label="Metric A", color="steelblue") ax.plot(df["month"], df["metric_b"], marker="s", label="Metric B", color="coral") ax.set_title("[YOUR CHART TITLE]", fontsize=14, fontweight="bold") ax.set_xlabel("Month") ax.set_ylabel("[Unit]") ax.legend() ax.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5) plt.tight_layout() plt.show() In [7]: Copied! 
# Placeholder point locations
# Replace with your own lat/lon data, e.g. from a GeoDataFrame or CSV
sites = pd.DataFrame({
    "name":  ["Site A", "Site B", "Site C", "Site D", "Site E"],
    "lat":   [28.61,    28.65,    28.58,    28.70,    28.55],
    "lon":   [77.20,    77.23,    77.17,    77.28,    77.15],
    "value": [142,      168,      95,       210,       87],
})

# Centre the map on the mean location
m = folium.Map(
    location=[sites["lat"].mean(), sites["lon"].mean()],
    zoom_start=11,
    tiles="CartoDB positron",
)

# Add a CircleMarker for each site, scaled by value
for _, row in sites.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row["lat"], row["lon"]],
        radius=row["value"] / 20,          # scale radius to value
        color="steelblue",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"{row['name']}: {row['value']}",
        popup=folium.Popup(
            f"<b>{row['name']}</b><br>Value: {row['value']}",
            max_width=200,
        ),
    ).add_to(m)

m
